perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party shall disseminate Confidential Information only to those employees, independent contractors, advisors, or Affiliates, on a “need to know” basis in order for Receiving Party to carry out the Purpose, and Receiving Party warrants that all such employees, independent contractors, advisors, or Affiliates shall be advised of the confidential nature of the information received and that all such employees, independent contractors, advisors, Affiliates shall be bound in writing by obligations no less stringent than the terms set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party shall disseminate Confidential Information freely to its employees, independent contractors, advisors, and Affiliates, without restriction, in order for Receiving Party to carry out the Purpose, and Receiving Party makes no guarantees on whether these parties will treat the information as confidential.
perturbation.explanation: This creates a direct in-text contradiction. It first allows for the dissemination of information without restriction, and then provides no guarantees on confidentiality. This contradicts the overall intention of the agreement to protect confidential information, and creates uncertainty around the legal enforcement of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) Use; Disclosure . Receiving Party shall use the Confidential Information solely for the Purpose defined above. Receiving Party shall not use the Confidential Information for any other purpose, including but not limited to using it in connection with the development or commercialization of any process or product, or using it in connection with any submission to any governmental agency, including any patent office or regulatory authority, or the like, without the express written permission of Disclosing Party. Receiving Party shall disseminate Confidential Information freely to its employees, independent contractors, advisors, and Affiliates, without restriction, in order for Receiving Party to carry out the Purpose, and Receiving Party makes no guarantees on whether these parties will treat the information as confidential. Receiving Party agrees to notify Disclosing Party immediately in writing upon any loss, misuse, misappropriation, or other unauthorized disclosure of the Confidential Information of Disclosing Party that may come to Receiving Party’s attention.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Receiving Party must ensure that employees, contractors, advisors, and Affiliates are bound by obligations no less stringent than the terms of the agreement. The changed text allows for the dissemination of confidential information 'without restriction' and provides 'no guarantees' on whether these parties will treat the information as confidential. This directly contradicts the requirement in the original text and in the 'Contradicted Text' which requires the receiving party to use the confidential information solely for the purpose defined and immediately notify the disclosing party of any unauthorized disclosure. The change introduces a clear inconsistency and contradiction regarding the protection and handling of confidential information, fitting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party shall hold the Confidential Information in strict confidence, and shall take all reasonable precautions to protect the Confidential Information at all times from unauthorized disclosure, publication, or use, including, without limitation, using at least the same degree of care as it employs to protect its own Confidential Information of like nature (but in any event no less than a reasonable degree of care), acting in a manner consistent with its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party shall hold the Confidential Information with a level of care determined solely by the Receiving Party, and may or may not take precautions to protect the Confidential Information at all times from unauthorized disclosure, publication, or use.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the intention of the agreement. The new language gives the Receiving Party full discretion over the level of care and whether or not to take precautions, contradicting the original text, creating uncertainty about the enforceability of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Degree of Care . Receiving Party shall hold the Confidential Information with a level of care determined solely by the Receiving Party, and may or may not take precautions to protect the Confidential Information at all times from unauthorized disclosure, publication, or use, including, without limitation, using at least the same degree of care as it employs to protect its own Confidential Information of like nature (but in any event no less than a reasonable degree of care), acting in a manner consistent with its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the Receiving Party can determine the level of care and may or may not take precautions to protect the Confidential Information. The contradicted text (which is the same as the Changed Text) now stands in contrast to the original obligation, which required a reasonable degree of care and actions consistent with the agreement. This creates a direct contradiction and uncertainty, impacting enforceability, as the two texts provide conflicting guidance on the level of care required, fulfilling the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Immediately upon (a) termination or expiration of this Agreement, (b) the decision by either Party not to enter into the business or scientific relationship contemplated above, or (c) a request by Disclosing Party at any time, Receiving Party will promptly turn over to Disclosing Party, or destroy, all Confidential Information of Disclosing Party and all documents, media, and other tangible materials containing any such Confidential Information and any and all extracts thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Immediately upon (a) termination or expiration of this Agreement, (b) the decision by either Party not to enter into the business or scientific relationship contemplated above, or (c) a request by Disclosing Party at any time, Receiving Party may or may not turn over to Disclosing Party, or destroy, all Confidential Information of Disclosing Party and all documents, media, and other tangible materials containing any such Confidential Information and any and all extracts thereof. The receiving party has sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The added 'may or may not' significantly changes the obligation to return or destroy confidential information. This introduces a contradiction because it is no longer mandatory, which contrasts with the underlying purpose of a non-disclosure agreement to ensure information protection. This creates uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (f) Return of Materials . Immediately upon (a) termination or expiration of this Agreement, (b) the decision by either Party not to enter into the business or scientific relationship contemplated above, or (c) a request by Disclosing Party at any time, Receiving Party may or may not turn over to Disclosing Party, or destroy, all Confidential Information of Disclosing Party and all documents, media, and other tangible materials containing any such Confidential Information and any and all extracts thereof. In the event that Receiving Party destroys Confidential Information, upon the destruction thereof, Receiving Party will issue to Disclosing Party a certificate as proof of compliance with Disclosing Party’s request. Notwithstanding this Section 4(f) , the Receiving Party shall not be required to purge Confidential Information from its computer system’s historical back-up media, provided that such Confidential Information that is retained will remain subject to the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies a mandatory obligation ('will promptly turn over') to a discretionary one ('may or may not turn over'). This directly contradicts the purpose and effect of the original text, which aimed to ensure mandatory return or destruction of confidential information. The contradicted text is effectively the same clause, therefore the change creates a direct in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Inconsistencies' perturbation type because the modification creates conflicting obligations within the contract regarding the return of confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party agrees that disclosure of Confidential Information without the express written permission of Disclosing Party will cause Disclosing Party irreparable harm and that any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement by Receiving Party will entitle Disclosing Party to injunctive relief, in addition to any other legal and/or equitable remedies available to it.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party agrees that disclosure of Confidential Information without the express written permission of Disclosing Party may or may not cause Disclosing Party irreparable harm and that any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement by Receiving Party may or may not entitle Disclosing Party to injunctive relief, in addition to any other legal and/or equitable remedies available to it.
perturbation.explanation: The original text makes a definitive statement that unauthorized disclosure will cause irreparable harm and entitle the disclosing party to injunctive relief. The modified text uses “may or may not,” creating a direct contradiction that weakens the clause and introduces ambiguity and makes enforcement uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (g) Injunctive Relief . Receiving Party agrees that disclosure of Confidential Information without the express written permission of Disclosing Party may or may not cause Disclosing Party irreparable harm and that any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement by Receiving Party may or may not entitle Disclosing Party to injunctive relief, in addition to any other legal and/or equitable remedies available to it. Notwithstanding clause (f) , Disclosing Party may seek injunctive relief in any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The modified text changes a definitive statement regarding irreparable harm and entitlement to injunctive relief to a conditional one ('may or may not'). This directly contradicts the original intent of the clause, weakening it and introducing ambiguity. Since the Contradicted Text is the same as the Changed Text, this creates a direct contradiction within the document, making enforcement uncertain. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
